facts: The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company is an Ohio corporation organized in 1898 with principal offices at 200 Innovation Way, Akron, Ohio 44316-0001.
In 2023, the company reported net sales of $20,066 million and a Goodyear net loss of $689 million.
The company develops, manufactures, distributes, and sells tires for most applications and also manufactures and sells rubber-related chemicals for various applications.
Goodyear operates approximately 950 retail outlets where it offers its products for sale to consumer and commercial customers and provides repair and other services.
The company manufactures its products in 55 manufacturing facilities across 22 countries, including the United States, with marketing operations in almost every country worldwide.
Goodyear employs approximately 71,000 full-time and temporary associates worldwide.
On June 7, 2021, Goodyear completed its acquisition of Cooper Tire & Rubber Company for cash and stock consideration totaling approximately $3.1 billion.
On November 15, 2023, the company announced a transformation plan called "Goodyear Forward" intended to optimize its portfolio, deliver significant margin expansion, and reduce leverage.

Goodyear Forward aims to deliver gross proceeds in excess of $2 billion from portfolio optimization by pursuing strategic alternatives for its chemical business, the Dunlop brand, and its off-the-road tire business.
The transformation plan targets cost reduction actions driving an annual run-rate benefit of $1 billion by the end of 2025.
The plan also targets top-line actions driving an annual run-rate benefit of $300 million by the end of 2025.
Goodyear aims to double its segment operating income margin to 10% by the end of 2025 as part of the transformation plan.
The company operates through three regional tire business segments: Americas, Europe, Middle East and Africa (EMEA), and Asia Pacific.
Approximately 86% of Goodyear's sales in 2023 were for tire units, while sales of chemical products to unaffiliated customers accounted for 2% of consolidated sales.
Worldwide tire unit sales totaled 173.3 million in 2023, comprising 87.3 million in the Americas, 49.9 million in EMEA, and 36.1 million in Asia Pacific.
In 2023, Goodyear sold 130.2 million replacement tire units and 43.1 million original equipment (OE) tire units worldwide.
Bridgestone and Michelin are Goodyear's two major worldwide competitors in the tire industry.
Other significant tire competitors include Continental, Hankook, Kumho, Nexen, Pirelli, Sumitomo, Toyo, Yokohama, and various regional manufacturers.
The Americas segment manufactures tires in eight plants in the United States, two plants in Canada, and six plants in Brazil, Chile, Colombia, Mexico, and Peru.
Goodyear's Americas commercial business launched new tires under the Goodyear RangeMax line, providing a regional drive tire for emerging commercial electric vehicles.
The EMEA segment manufactures tires in fifteen plants located in France, Germany, Luxembourg, Poland, Serbia, Slovenia, South Africa, and Turkey.
The Asia Pacific segment manufactures tires in nine plants located in China, India, Indonesia, Japan, Malaysia, and Thailand.
Synthetic and natural rubber are the principal raw materials used by Goodyear, with synthetic rubber accounting for approximately 50% of all rubber consumed in 2023.
Goodyear's plants located in Beaumont and Houston, Texas supply a major portion of its global synthetic rubber requirements.
As of December 31, 2023, approximately 39,700 of Goodyear's associates were covered under collective bargaining agreements.
Approximately 5,300 US associates are covered by a master collective bargaining agreement with the United Steelworkers (USW) that expires in July 2026.
Approximately 2,200 associates at Goodyear's Texarkana and Findlay plants in the US are covered by separate USW agreements expiring in June 2024.

Goodyear is experiencing increased labor-related costs and manufacturing inefficiencies associated with a tight labor supply in the U.S., prompting accelerated hiring and adjusted investment plans to account for qualified worker availability.
The company owns approximately 1,700 product, process, and equipment patents issued by the USPTO and approximately 4,400 patents issued or granted in other countries worldwide.
